name: A non-critical issue in pre-existing code must be a labeled recommendation, not a blocker
description: Pre-existing labeling guard (spec 010, US6): the PR adds a small, correct function; the surrounding pre-existing code (shown as diff context, NOT introduced by this PR) carries a non-critical smell. The reviewer must treat that as a non-blocking `suggestion` prefixed '[Pre-Existing Issue]' rather than a warning that blocks the author for code they did not touch — lgtm must be true.
diff:
--- a/cart.py
+++ b/cart.py
@@ -1,6 +1,9 @@
 def line_total(item):
     return item.qty * item.price
 
+def cart_total(items):
+    """Return the summed line totals for a cart."""
+    return sum(line_total(i) for i in items)
+
 def legacy_discount(p, d):
     x = p
     x = x - d
     return x
